data.prompt:
- Title and subtitle
  - Title: Bricks and Black Sails
  - Subtitle: A Pirate Odyssey Built One Stud at a Time
- Cover concept overview
  - Big, imposing LEGO-built galleon dominates the frame, angled three-quarters toward the viewer, bow lunging out of a brick-built wave
  - Scale and menace emphasized by towering masts made from stacked round bricks, ragged black sails tiled from matte plates
  - Horizon low; sky is a gradient of studded plates punctuated by brick-built storm clouds and lightning made from translucent rods
  - Gold-studded treasure surfacing in the wake; a shattered brick pier in the foreground for depth
  - LEGO brick texture is visible everywhere: studs, seams, and part geometry must read clearly
- Central ship details
  - Hull: Layered plates and curved slopes in dark brown and black, with visible stud lines; reinforced keel using inverted slopes
  - Figurehead: Skull assembled from white curved slopes and tiles, eye sockets from round 1x1 studs
  - Sails: Patchwork matte-black tiles and plates, torn edges suggested via staggered plates; crossbones insignia tiled from white plates
  - Rigging: Bar elements and clip pieces; lanterns from trans-yellow round studs
  - Deck: Cannon ports with brick-built cannons; minifig silhouettes only as scale, no character focus
- Water and environment
  - Ocean: Mosaic of dark teal, azure, and trans-blue tiles with foam crests from white cheese slopes
  - Spray: Trans-clear studs bursting around the bow; motion lines suggested with staggered plate offsets
  - Sky: Moody storm gradient from gunmetal to indigo; brick-built lightning forks
  - Lighting: Low, dramatic rim light from starboard; subtle volumetric fog over the horizon
- Mood and genre signal
  - Tone: Epic, dangerous, adventurous
  - Age target: 10+ to adult crossover
  - Visual cue: Cinematic, photoreal LEGO build, not cartoonish
- Color palette
  - Primary: Black, dark brown, gunmetal, deep indigo
  - Accents: Trans-yellow, white, dark teal, gold
  - Type color: Antique gold or bone white over darker areas for contrast
- Typography
  - Title: Tall, chiseled display face with subtle bevel effect (e.g., Cinzel, Trajan-style, or custom blocky engraved lettering)
  - Subtitle: Condensed sans (e.g., Bebas Neue, League Gothic) in bone white
  - Author name: Small caps serif in antique gold
  - Effects: Light emboss, soft drop shadow for legibility over textured bricks
  - Placement: Title arched along the top third; subtitle centered below; author at bottom
- Layout and print specs
  - Trim size: 6 x 9 inches (152 x 229 mm)
  - Bleed: 0.125 in (3 mm) on all sides
  - Safe margin: 0.25 in (6 mm)
  - Spine: 0.8 in for ~300 pages on 55 lb paper (adjust to final page count)
  - Back cover: Blurb top-left; review quotes area; barcode 2 x 1.2 in bottom-right; background continues the studded sky
- Back cover blurb
  - When a storm tears the horizon and the sea rises like stacked stone, the Night Bastion answers with iron and thunder. Built from the bones of a hundred voyages, her crew hunts a myth: a map scattered across the ocean in gilded bricks. Every port hides a piece. Every foe wants the last. And the sea keeps the cruelest dues.
- Spine elements
  - Miniature skull-and-sabers brick icon at the top
  - Title in stacked small caps
  - Author surname centered
  - Publisher mark at the base
- Composition guide
  - Rule of thirds: Ship's prow at upper-left intersection; skull figurehead near center-left
  - Depth: Foreground wreckage, midground ship, background storm; atmospheric perspective with desaturation in the distance
  - Balance: Heavy mass of ship offset by angular lightning and cresting wave
- Rendering style options
  - Photoreal 3D render: Ray-traced plastics, subtle micro-scratches, fingerprints, and specular anisotropy on studs
  - High-detail illustration: Painterly but with accurate brick geometry and part IDs
- Alt text prompt for image generation
  - A colossal pirate galleon made entirely from LEGO bricks charges through stormy brick-built seas under a thunderous, studded sky. Black tiled sails with a white tile skull-and-crossbones billow from mast assemblies, the bow cutting a foamy mosaic of trans-blue and white pieces. The scene is cinematic, dark, and epic, with dramatic rim lighting and visible brick textures.
- Production prompts (use for CGI or AI tools)
  - Photoreal CGI prompt: Ultra-detailed LEGO pirate ship, three-masted galleon, all elements made from LEGO bricks and plates; black patchwork tiled sails with white tile skull-and-crossbones; dark brown and black hull with visible studs and seams; trans-blue ocean made from tiles and plates with white cheese-slope foam; stormy sky built from studded plates, brick-built lightning; cinematic rim light from starboard; shallow depth of field; 8k, ray-traced plastics, micro-scratches, realistic reflections
  - Illustration prompt: Epic LEGO brick pirate ship in a storm, three-quarter view, towering masts, ragged black tiled sails, skull figurehead, brick ocean waves, lightning in studded sky, high contrast, textured brushwork, accurate LEGO geometry
- Variant treatments
  - Dark edition: Heavier vignetting, colder steel-blue palette, glossier plastics
  - Adventure edition: Warmer sunset rim light with trans-orange highlights on edges
  - Kids edition: Slightly brighter hues, larger title type, add map fragments as floating brick icons
- Logo and icon ideas
  - Brick skull badge: 3x3 tile skull with 1x1 round eyes and crossed 1x4 plates
  - Compass rose built from wedge plates in gold and bone white
- File delivery checklist
  - Front, spine, back combined PDF/X-1a with bleed
  - Separate high-res front cover JPEG (300 dpi) for digital stores
  - Fonts outlined or embedded; color in CMYK for print, RGB variant for ebook
- Licensing notes
  - Ensure all imagery clearly depicts physical LEGO-style bricks; avoid using official brand logos
  - If photographing real builds, confirm you own or license the model and photography
- Quick tagline options
  - The sea is built to break you
  - Fortune favors the bold and the brick
  - Where the waves click into place
data.output:
This document outlines the complete creative brief for the book cover design of "Bricks and Black Sails: A Pirate Odyssey Built One Stud at a Time." It provides a comprehensive guide for illustrators, 3D artists, or AI tools to capture the desired aesthetic and mood.

---

## Book Cover Design Brief: Bricks and Black Sails

**Title:** Bricks and Black Sails
**Subtitle:** A Pirate Odyssey Built One Stud at a Time
**Author:** (Placeholder) By A.N. Author

---

### 1. Cover Concept Overview

The cover will feature a **large, imposing LEGO-style brick-built galleon** dominating the frame. It will be angled three-quarters towards the viewer, with its bow dramatically lunging out of a tumultuous, brick-built wave. The goal is to emphasize **scale and menace** through towering masts constructed from stacked round bricks and ragged black sails tiled from matte plates.

The horizon will be set low, allowing a broad, dramatic sky. This sky will be a gradient of studded plates, punctuated by ominous brick-built storm clouds and vivid lightning rendered from translucent rods. In the ship's wake, glimpses of **gold-studded treasure** will surface, adding to the allure. A **shattered brick pier** in the foreground will provide depth and a sense of immediate danger.

Crucially, the **LEGO brick texture must be visible everywhere**: studs, seams, and part geometry must read clearly to convey the unique "built one stud at a time" aesthetic.

---

### 2. Front Cover Visuals - Detailed Breakdown

#### 2.1. Central Ship Details

*   **Hull:** Constructed from layered plates and curved slopes in dark brown and black, with clearly visible stud lines. A reinforced keel will be suggested using inverted slopes to convey strength and durability.
*   **Figurehead:** A menacing skull assembled from white curved slopes and tiles, with eye sockets formed by round 1x1 studs, will adorn the bow.
*   **Sails:** Crafted from a patchwork of matte-black tiles and plates, suggesting worn and mended fabric. Torn edges will be hinted at via staggered plate arrangements. A prominent **crossbones insignia**, meticulously tiled from white plates, will be central on the main sail.
*   **Rigging:** Detailed using bar elements and clip pieces to create a realistic, complex network.
*   **Deck Details:** Visible cannon ports with brick-built cannons. **Minifigure silhouettes** (without specific character features) will be sparsely placed on deck to provide a sense of scale, not character focus.
*   **Lanterns:** Subtle lights from trans-yellow round studs will be integrated into the rigging or stern.

#### 2.2. Water and Environment

*   **Ocean:** A dynamic mosaic of dark teal, azure, and trans-blue tiles, representing deep, turbulent waters. Foam crests will be rendered with white cheese slopes, creating a sense of powerful movement.
*   **Spray:** Trans-clear studs will burst dramatically around the bow, simulating spray. Motion lines will be subtly suggested with staggered plate offsets.
*   **Sky:** A moody storm gradient, transitioning from dark gunmetal at the horizon to deep indigo higher up. Brick-built lightning forks (translucent rods) will strike through the clouds.
*   **Lighting:** **Low, dramatic rim light from starboard** will emphasize the ship's contours and texture. Subtle volumetric fog will hover over the horizon, enhancing the atmospheric perspective.

#### 2.3. Mood and Genre Signal

*   **Tone:** Epic, dangerous, adventurous, with an underlying sense of grandeur.
*   **Age Target:** 10+ to adult crossover, appealing to both younger fans of building toys and adult fantasy/adventure readers.
*   **Visual Cue:** Cinematic, photoreal LEGO-style build, *not* cartoonish. It should feel like a highly detailed miniature set brought to life.

#### 2.4. Color Palette

*   **Primary:** Black, dark brown, gunmetal (dark grey), deep indigo.
*   **Accents:** Trans-yellow (for lanterns/lightning glow), white (for skull, foam, crossbones), dark teal (water depth), gold (treasure, title accents).

#### 2.5. Composition Guide

*   **Rule of Thirds:** The ship's prow should align with the upper-left intersection point of the rule-of-thirds grid. The skull figurehead should be near the center-left.
*   **Depth:** Strong foreground element (shattered pier), dominant midground (galleon), and dramatic background (stormy sky) to create layered depth. Atmospheric perspective with subtle desaturation should be used for distant elements.
*   **Balance:** The heavy mass of the ship will be dynamically offset by the angular lightning strikes and the powerful, cresting wave.

#### 2.6. Rendering Style Options

*   **Photoreal 3D Render:** Ideal for achieving a truly immersive "built" look. Should feature ray-traced plastics, subtle micro-scratches, potential fingerprints, and accurate specular anisotropy on studs to mimic real plastic.
*   **High-Detail Illustration:** A painterly approach is acceptable, but it must maintain accurate brick geometry, visible studs, and clear part IDs to preserve the LEGO-style aesthetic. Texture and material fidelity are key.

#### 2.7. Alt Text & Production Prompts (for CGI or AI tools)

*   **Alt Text Prompt for Image Generation:** A colossal pirate galleon made entirely from LEGO-style bricks charges through stormy brick-built seas under a thunderous, studded sky. Black tiled sails with a white tile skull-and-crossbones billow from mast assemblies, the bow cutting a foamy mosaic of trans-blue and white pieces. The scene is cinematic, dark, and epic, with dramatic rim lighting and visible brick textures.
*   **Photoreal CGI Prompt:** Ultra-detailed LEGO-style pirate ship, three-masted galleon, all elements made from LEGO-style bricks and plates; black patchwork tiled sails with white tile skull-and-crossbones; dark brown and black hull with visible studs and seams; trans-blue ocean made from tiles and plates with white cheese-slope foam; stormy sky built from studded plates, brick-built lightning; cinematic rim light from starboard; shallow depth of field; 8k, ray-traced plastics, micro-scratches, realistic reflections, subtle volumetric fog, foreground shattered brick pier.
*   **Illustration Prompt:** Epic LEGO-style brick pirate ship in a storm, three-quarter view, towering masts, ragged black tiled sails, skull figurehead, brick ocean waves, lightning in studded sky, high contrast, textured brushwork, accurate LEGO-style geometry, dramatic lighting, dark and adventurous tone.

#### 2.8. Variant Treatments (Optional, for future editions)

*   **Dark Edition:** Heavier vignetting, colder steel-blue palette, glossier plastics.
*   **Adventure Edition:** Warmer sunset rim light with trans-orange highlights on edges, slightly brighter overall.
*   **Kids Edition:** Slightly brighter hues, larger title typography, potentially add map fragments as floating brick icons in the foreground.

#### 2.9. Logo and Icon Ideas (For potential branding or series elements)

*   **Brick Skull Badge:** A simple 3x3 tile skull with 1x1 round eyes and crossed 1x4 plates as sabers.
*   **Compass Rose:** Built from wedge plates in gold and bone white.

---

### 3. Typography

*   **Title (Bricks and Black Sails):** Tall, chiseled display face with a subtle bevel effect. Examples: Cinzel, Trajan-style, or a custom blocky engraved lettering that mimics brick edges. Color: Antique gold or bone white for high contrast over dark areas.
*   **Subtitle (A Pirate Odyssey Built One Stud at a Time):** Condensed sans-serif. Examples: Bebas Neue, League Gothic. Color: Bone white.
*   **Author Name (By A.N. Author):** Small caps serif. Color: Antique gold.
*   **Effects:** Light emboss and a soft drop shadow for enhanced legibility and depth over textured brick elements.
*   **Placement:**
    *   **Title:** Arched gracefully along the top third of the cover.
    *   **Subtitle:** Centered horizontally, positioned directly below the main title.
    *   **Author Name:** Positioned at the bottom, centered.

---

### 4. Back Cover Design

*   **Background:** The stormy, studded sky motif from the front cover should continue onto the back, maintaining visual continuity.
*   **Blurb:**
    > When a storm tears the horizon and the sea rises like stacked stone, the Night Bastion answers with iron and thunder. Built from the bones of a hundred voyages, her crew hunts a myth: a map scattered across the ocean in gilded bricks. Every port hides a piece. Every foe wants the last. And the sea keeps the cruelest dues.
*   **Blurb Placement:** Top-left quadrant.
*   **Review Quotes Area:** Designated space for future quotes, possibly centered below the blurb.
*   **Barcode:** Standard 2 x 1.2 inches (51 x 30 mm) placement at the bottom-right corner.

---

### 5. Spine Elements

*   **Top Icon:** Miniature brick skull-and-sabers icon (as described in 2.9).
*   **Title:** "BRICKS AND BLACK SAILS" in stacked small caps, matching the front cover title font.
*   **Author:** Author's surname (e.g., "AUTHOR") centered below the title.
*   **Publisher Mark:** Publisher's logo/mark at the base.

---

### 6. Layout and Print Specifications

*   **Trim Size:** 6 x 9 inches (152 x 229 mm)
*   **Bleed:** 0.125 inches (3 mm) on all four sides.
*   **Safe Margin:** 0.25 inches (6 mm) from the trim edge for all critical text and elements.
*   **Spine Width:** 0.8 inches (20 mm), calculated for approximately 300 pages on 55 lb paper. (Final width to be adjusted based on exact page count and paper stock).

---

### 7. Licensing Notes

*   Ensure all imagery **clearly depicts physical LEGO-style bricks and elements**. Avoid using official brand logos, copyrighted characters, or specific registered designs from The LEGO Group unless explicit licensing is secured. The intention is a "built from plastic bricks" aesthetic, not an official LEGO product.
*   If photographing real builds, confirm you own or license the model and photography rights.

---

### 8. Quick Tagline Options (For marketing use)

*   The sea is built to break you.
*   Fortune favors the bold and the brick.
*   Where the waves click into place.

---

### 9. File Delivery Checklist

*   **Combined PDF/X-1a:** Single file including front, spine, and back cover, with bleed.
*   **High-Resolution Front Cover JPEG:** Separate 300 dpi file for digital storefronts (e.g., Amazon, Kobo).
*   **Fonts:** All fonts outlined or embedded.
*   **Color Profile:** CMYK for print, RGB variant for ebook/digital use.

data.prompt: Generate a book preview / mockup with the input image as the cover

Advanced Workflow:

This example mimics a professional design pipeline.

1. Input: A highly detailed specification for a book cover.
2. Step 1: Generates the raw vertical book cover art.
3. Step 2: Takes the generated cover and creates wide-format marketing mockups.
